title: Windows Podman is using the default user to control created machines
body:
/kind bug



**Description**

It looks like windows podman is using the default user to control created machines/distros. I hit a [WSL issue](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/8573) when trying to run vscode from the Linux command line inside a podman machine. So I tried running vscode from the PowerShell command line:

    code --remote wsl+podman-westie /home/user

This did open vscode in the desired directory but ran as root. I added a wsl.conf directory in /etc/ setting the default user. Rerunning the above command line did open vscode in the podman machine at the correct directory and running as user "user". However later I tried to restart the machine using "podman machine start westie" and it failed with a permissions error. It seems like the podman command line should specify the root user when sending wsl requests


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a wsl.conf file specifying default user as "user"

2. Run podman machine start <machinename>

**Describe the results you received:**

	PS C:\Users\mlwp> podman machine start westie
	Starting machine "westie"
	/bin/bash: line 1: /root/bootstrap: Permission denied
	Error: WSL bootstrap script failed: exit status 126

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman machine should be started

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

comments.body:
Similar topic: https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/14571

While ideally our WSL integration could be enhanced so that the wsl prompt takes you in as a standard user, its likely to still have problems if vscode is using wsl.exe to directly execute commands (most likely). The issue is that podman is running inside of systemd inside of a nested namespace we create. We can use /etc/profile.d/ to auto enter a login from wsl, but in the case of a direct command execution, bash is bypassed. If they allow you to customize the wsl command we have a wrapper script called enterns that can be used (e.g. wsl enterns podman info). I haven't looked at it to know if they have any flexibility here.

It might be possible to get it working if this is launched from inside wsl (e.g. code .) 

The remote ssh or container backends to vscode might be worth exploring until we have a solution.
